evals.prompt: I have a Python function that answers questions using a local LLM. I want to run it as a container-based Foundry Agent so my team can call it through Azure AI Foundry. Show me how to wrap it with FoundryCBAgent, expose the /responses endpoint, and register it in Foundry with HostedAgentDefinition. My container image will be myacr.azurecr.io/my-llm-agent:latest and I want to give it 2 CPUs and 4Gi of memory.
evals.expected_output: Complete code showing: (1) FoundryCBAgent setup with agent_run implementation that calls the user's function, (2) agent.run() to start server, (3) AIProjectClient with allow_preview=True, (4) create_version call with HostedAgentDefinition including container_protocol_versions with AgentProtocol.RESPONSES v0.1.1, cpu='2', memory='4Gi', and the image URL

assertions.name: uses_foundry_cb_agent
assertions.description: Code imports and instantiates FoundryCBAgent from azure.ai.agentserver.core

assertions.name: uses_agent_run_context
assertions.description: Code uses AgentRunContext for accessing user input

assertions.name: correct_cpu_memory
assertions.description: cpu and memory values are set correctly

assertions.name: correct_image
assertions.description: Image URL from the prompt is used

evals.prompt: I want to build a streaming container agent for Azure AI Foundry. My agent should stream responses token by token. Show me how to implement the streaming pattern with FoundryCBAgent — I need the right event types and the correct event ordering. Also show how to test it locally with curl.
evals.expected_output: Code showing: async generator returned from agent_run, ResponseCreatedEvent followed by ResponseOutputItemAddedEvent, then ResponseTextDeltaEvent for each chunk, then ResponseTextDoneEvent, then ResponseCompletedEvent. Plus curl command for local streaming test.

assertions.name: uses_text_delta_event
assertions.description: Code includes ResponseTextDeltaEvent for streaming tokens

assertions.name: curl_streaming_test
assertions.description: Includes curl command for local testing with stream:true

evals.prompt: I already have an Azure AI Foundry container agent registered. Show me how to start a multi-turn conversation with it using the Conversations + Responses API. I want to ask it two questions and keep the conversation context between them, then clean up the conversation.
evals.expected_output: Code showing: AIProjectClient setup, get_openai_client(), conversations.create() with initial message, responses.create() with agent_reference dict, conversations.items.create() for second message, second responses.create(), and conversations.delete() for cleanup.

assertions.name: uses_get_openai_client
assertions.description: Code calls get_openai_client() on the AIProjectClient

assertions.name: creates_conversation
assertions.description: Code calls conversations.create() to start a conversation

assertions.name: multi_turn_continuation
assertions.description: Code adds a second message to the same conversation

assertions.name: conversation_cleanup
assertions.description: Code deletes the conversation at the end
